tool_name: Machining Chatter Stability, Surface Roughness & Tool Deflection Analysis

inputs.name: Tool Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Feed per Tooth (fz)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Number of Cutting Edges (z)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Tool Nose Radius (rε)
inputs.hint: From insert catalogue — required for Ra calculation (ISO 3002)

inputs.name: Radial Depth of Cut (ae)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Tool Overhang Length (L)
inputs.hint: Distance from spindle nose to tool tip — critical for deflection

inputs.name: Drawing Ra Specification (target)
inputs.hint: From engineering drawing: Ra 0.8 / Ra 1.6 / Ra 3.2 — ISO 1302
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Machine / Fixture Rigidity Class
options: HIGH — Heavy machining centre, short toolholder, vise
MEDIUM — Standard VMC, standard extension, milling fixture
LOW — Long overhang, thin wall part, weak fixture, worn spindle
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Rigidity Dynamic Factor (Kd)
inputs.hint: HIGH=1.0 | MEDIUM=1.4–1.8 | LOW=2.0–3.5 — amplifies Rz by chatter

inputs.name: Toolholder / Shank Material
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Shank Elastic Modulus (E)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Elastic modulus (steel): CoV=0.03

inputs.name: Estimated Tangential Cutting Force (Fc)
inputs.hint: From PRO_043 output or Kienzle calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: Vf_feed_rate = feed_per_tooth_fz * num_teeth_z * n_rpm   // [mm/min] Table feed rate
Rz_theoretical = (POWER(feed_per_tooth_fz, 2) / (8 * nose_radius_mm)) * 1000   // [μm] Theoretical peak-to-valley roughness — ISO 3002
Rz_actual = Rz_theoretical * rigidity_factor   // [μm] Actual Rz incl. chatter amplification
Ra_approx = Rz_actual / 4.5   // [μm] Approximate Ra (end milling, k=4.5) — ISO 3002
L_D_ratio = overhang_length_mm / tool_diameter_mm   // [—] Overhang-to-diameter ratio — chatter risk indicator
I_moment = (3.14159 * POWER(tool_diameter_mm, 4)) / 64   // [mm⁴] Second moment of area — circular cross section
Tool_Deflection_mm = (cutting_force_fc_n * POWER(overhang_length_mm, 3)) / (3 * elastic_modulus_mpa * I_moment)   // [mm] Static tool tip deflection — cantilever beam model
Chatter_Risk_Index = L_D_ratio * rigidity_factor   // [—] Composite chatter risk — higher = worse
Dimensional_Error_um = Tool_Deflection_mm * 1000   // [μm] Dimensional error from deflection
Safe_fz_for_Ra = SQRT(8 * nose_radius_mm * ra_specification_um / 1000 / rigidity_factor)   // [mm/tooth] Max fz to achieve Ra spec

nose_radius_minimum.error_msg: Nose radius (rε) cannot be zero — division by zero in Ra calculation. Minimum: 0.01mm. Check insert catalogue.

ae_exceeds_diameter.error_msg: Radial depth (ae) exceeds tool diameter — geometrically impossible. ae must be ≤ D.

ap_exceeds_limit.error_msg: Axial depth (ap) exceeds 80% of tool diameter. Catastrophic tool breakage risk. Reduce ap.

rigidity_factor_range.error_msg: Dynamic rigidity factor (Kd) cannot be less than 1.0. Ideal (chatter-free) conditions = 1.0.

smart_warnings.message: SURFACE FINISH VIOLATION: Predicted Ra exceeds drawing specification. Reduce fz or use a wiper insert (larger rε). Safe fz for spec compliance is shown below.

CHATTER RISK HIGH — L/D > 4: At this overhang ratio, regenerative chatter is likely. Symptoms: harmonic surface marks, tool noise, premature wear. Mitigations: (1) Use anti-vibration toolholder, (2) Reduce ap by 30–40%, (3) Change spindle speed ±10% to shift from resonance zone.
DIMENSIONAL INACCURACY: Tool tip deflection exceeds 0.05mm (50μm). Workpiece dimensional tolerance will be violated. Reduce overhang, increase tool diameter, or reduce cutting force.
Elevated tool deflection (20–50μm): Monitor dimensional output. For tight tolerances (IT6 or better), this deflection will cause out-of-spec parts. Consider shorter toolholder or reduced Fc.
Tooth passing frequency > 1000 Hz: May excite spindle bearing natural frequencies. If chatter occurs, shift RPM by ±5–10% or change number of flutes to detune from structural resonance.

COMBINED HIGH-RISK: Weak fixture + long overhang simultaneously. Predicted Ra and deflection values are unreliable — actual results may be significantly worse. Mandatory: reduce overhang to L/D ≤ 3 before proceeding.

fmea.effect: Radial depth (ae) exceeds tool diameter — geometrically impossible. ae must be ≤ D.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Axial depth (ap) exceeds 80% of tool diameter. Catastrophic tool breakage risk. Reduce ap.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Dynamic rigidity factor (Kd) cannot be less than 1.0. Ideal (chatter-free) conditions = 1.0.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: SURFACE FINISH VIOLATION: Predicted Ra exceeds drawing specification. Reduce fz or use a wiper insert (larger rε). Safe fz for spec compliance is shown below.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_approx > ra_specification_um

fmea.failureMode: CHATTER RISK HIGH — L/D > 4: At this overhang ratio, regenerative chatter is likely. Symptoms: harmonic surface marks, tool noise, premature wear. Mitigations: (1) Use anti-vibration toolholder, (2) Reduce ap by 30–40%, (3) Change spindle speed ±10% to shift from resonance zone.
DIMENSIONAL INACCURACY: Tool tip deflection exceeds 0.05mm (50μm). Workpiece dimensional tolerance will be violated. Reduce overhang, increase tool diameter, or reduce cutting force.
Elevated tool deflection (20–50μm): Monitor dimensional output. For tight tolerances (IT6 or better), this deflection will cause out-of-spec parts. Consider shorter toolholder or reduced Fc.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tool_Deflection_mm > 0.02 && Tool_Deflection_mm <= 0.05

fmea.failureMode: Tooth passing frequency > 1000 Hz: May excite spindle bearing natural frequencies. If chatter occurs, shift RPM by ±5–10% or change number of flutes to detune from structural resonance.

COMBINED HIGH-RISK: Weak fixture + long overhang simultaneously. Predicted Ra and deflection values are unreliable — actual results may be significantly worse. Mandatory: reduce overhang to L/D ≤ 3 before proceeding.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rigidity_factor > 2.0 && L_D_ratio > 3
